title: malus roris
intro: Malus roris, also known as the apple of snow or mountain ash, is a plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots possess medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Malus roris include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Malus roris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine for centuries to support overall well-being.
